summary.achievement: 1030x speedup for QTT 2D rendering via separable Kronecker contraction

gpu_tensor.description: CUDA tensor output, no CPU transfer

cpu_output.description: NumPy array output with CPU transfer

key_insight.title: Separable Kronecker Structure
key_insight.explanation: When x_cores and y_cores are disjoint, QTT has exact separable structure: value[x,y] = X_vec[x] @ Y_vec[y]. This reduces 2M contractions to 2K + 1 matmul.
key_insight.old_complexity: O(width Г— height Г— n_cores Г— rankВІ)
key_insight.new_complexity: O(2^n_x Г— n_x Г— rankВІ + 2^n_y Г— n_y Г— rankВІ + 2^n_x Г— 2^n_y)

separable_contraction.description: Exploit Kronecker structure of disjoint x/y cores

xy_vector_caching.description: Cache X_vecs and Y_vecs across frames for static viewports

fp16_gemm.description: Use half-precision for outer product matmul

gpu_tensor_output.description: Return CUDA tensor directly, skip CPU transfer

invariants: x_cores and y_cores must be disjoint (no overlap)
Call invalidate_cache() when QTT cores change
warnings: DO NOT use torch.compile - makes it 1000x slower
DO NOT loop over pixels - defeats the entire optimization
DO NOT call to_dense() on the QTT - will OOM
